TSLA: Michael O'Leary Thanks Elon Musk For The 'Wonderful' Publicity: Says Tesla CEO Free To Invest In Ryanair, Which Offers Better Returns Than X. Ryanair CEO Michael O'Leary stated that a public dispute with Elon Musk provided "wonderful" publicity for the airline, leading to a significant boost in bookings. O'Leary also invited Musk to invest in Ryanair, suggesting it would offer better returns than X, playfully noting that non-European citizens cannot own a majority of European airlines. The humorous exchange and O'Leary's comments followed Musk's criticism of Ryanair's decision not to install Starlink internet.

Lemonade launches Tesla FSD insurance with 50% discount – bigger than Tesla’s own. Lemonade has introduced "Autonomous Car Insurance," a new product that offers Tesla owners a significant 50% discount on per-mile insurance rates when Full Self-Driving (FSD) is engaged. This offering is substantially larger than the maximum 10% FSD discount provided by Tesla's own insurance. The initiative is a direct result of a technical collaboration allowing Lemonade access to Tesla's vehicle telemetry data, enabling them to differentiate between human-driven and FSD-driven miles.

Lemonade to cut insurance rates for Tesla drivers in endorsement of EV maker's software technology. Lemonade, a U.S. insurer, announced it would cut insurance rates by 50% for Tesla drivers when the Full Self-Driving (FSD) software is active, citing data that shows a reduction in accidents. This move acknowledges Tesla CEO Elon Musk's claims about the safety of their vehicle technology, despite regulatory concerns. The new "Autonomous Car insurance" offering will launch in Arizona and Oregon, with potential for further rate reductions as FSD software updates improve safety.

Jim Chanos Slams Tesla, Lemonade's Autonomous Insurance, Says True FSD Will Have Manufacturer's Liability: 'You Are All Being Played...'. Investor Jim Chanos has criticized the collaboration between Lemonade Inc. and Tesla Inc. regarding reduced insurance rates for FSD-engaged driving. Chanos argues that true Full Self-Driving (FSD) would involve manufacturer's liability insurance, making driver insurance unnecessary, and labels the current situation as a deceptive play by both companies. Despite Lemonade's claims of fewer accidents with FSD, concerns about safety persist, with lawsuits and a NHTSA probe questioning the system's deployment.

Nio Onvo launches HarmonyOS app for Huawei devices. Onvo, Nio's mass-market sub-brand, has launched a native HarmonyOS app for Huawei devices, following in the footsteps of the main Nio brand and Tesla. This move allows Onvo users to access services like remote vehicle control on Huawei's rapidly growing mobile operating system. Huawei's HarmonyOS has become one of the world's largest mobile operating systems after being sanctioned by the US and barred from using Google's GMS.

Michael O'Leary Thanks Elon Musk For The 'Wonderful' Publicity: Says Tesla CEO Free To Invest In Ryanair, Which Offers Better Returns Than X. Ryanair CEO Michael O'Leary has thanked Elon Musk for the "wonderful" publicity his recent public spat has generated for the airline, leading to a significant boost in bookings. O'Leary also extended an invitation for Musk to invest in Ryanair, suggesting it offers better returns than X, playfully noting that non-European citizens cannot hold a majority stake in European airlines. The comments came after Musk publicly criticized Ryanair's decision regarding Starlink internet and jokingly threatened to buy the airline and fire O'Leary.

Tesla Cybercab, Optimus early production to be ‘agonizingly slow,’ Musk says. Elon Musk has stated that early production of Tesla's Cybercab robotaxi and Optimus robot will be "agonizingly slow" due to the novelty of the projects, but expects production to become "insanely fast" eventually. These initiatives are seen as critical future growth drivers for Tesla, with Cybercab volume production targeted for 2026. The company is pivoting towards robotics and autonomous driving amidst declining EV sales and increased competition.

Cathie Wood's Ark Invest Estimates 'Eight Times' More Consumer Value Once Tesla Achieves This Critical Milestone. Ark Invest, led by Cathie Wood, projects that Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) service will offer customers eight times more value than its current subscription price once it achieves unsupervised autonomy. The firm believes the $99/month FSD subscription will be a "steal" as the technology matures, with Robotaxis potentially comprising 90% of Tesla's value by 2029. This forecast comes as Tesla makes progress on its 14th generation FSD and eyes new offerings like Cybercab, and as Ark Invest itself makes significant trades in the autonomous driving sector.

Tesla Says Free FSD Transfer Will End On March 31: Here's How You Can Qualify. Tesla has announced that its free Full Self-Driving (FSD) transfer service will conclude on March 31, requiring customers to place new vehicle orders by this date to qualify. This move comes as Tesla is expected to transition FSD to a subscription-only model after Valentine's Day, possibly to meet CEO Elon Musk's milestone of 10 million active FSD subscriptions for his pay package. Customers who order by the deadline can still take delivery after March 31 and retain the free FSD transfer.

This Analyst Calls Tesla 7 Times More 'Capital Efficient' Than Rivals: Here's Why. NewStreet Research analyst Pierre Ferragu states that Tesla is seven times more capital efficient than its rivals, particularly in its Robotaxi efforts compared to Waymo. Ferragu highlights Tesla's ability to leverage owners' personal cars for the Robotaxi network and its simpler sensor suite and lower manufacturing costs as key advantages. He also noted Waymo's current cost-per-mile is not competitive against companies like Uber, suggesting Uber could integrate Robotaxi providers to compete.

Safety monitors still tagging along on Tesla Robotaxi rides in Austin. Despite Elon Musk's promises that safety monitors would be removed from Tesla Robotaxis by the end of 2025, many rides in Austin continue to include a human safety monitor. While some truly driverless Tesla vehicles have been spotted, the majority of customer-hailed rides still have a person present, indicating that Tesla has not yet fully achieved its robotaxi goals. This situation contrasts with Waymo, which offers public driverless rides in Austin without safety monitors.
TGT: Morgan Stanley Raises Target (TGT) to $125, Reaffirms Overweight in Retail Sector Update. Morgan Stanley recently increased its price target for Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) to $125 from $112, maintaining an Overweight rating. This decision was part of their 2026 retail sector outlook, with other firms like Gordon Haskett also upgrading Target to a "Buy" rating. Target's consistent profitability, e-commerce investments, and strong dividend commitment continue to attract investor attention despite short-term challenges.

Morgan Stanley Raises Target (TGT) to $125, Reaffirms Overweight in Retail Sector Update. Morgan Stanley has raised its price target for Target Corporation (TGT) to $125 from $112, while maintaining an "Overweight" rating. This decision was part of their broader 2026 outlook for various retail sectors. The article also highlights increased investor attention on Target, a recent upgrade by Gordon Haskett to "Buy" with a $140 price target, and Target's consistent profitability and investment in e-commerce and in-store experiences despite current challenges.

Target Can’t Shake Acne Products Benzene Contamination Lawsuit. A federal judge has refused to dismiss most of a proposed class action lawsuit against Target Corp., alleging its Up&Up acne treatment products contain high levels of the carcinogen benzene. The court allowed consumer protection, false advertising, and implied warranty claims to proceed, rejecting Target's argument of federal preemption. This means consumers can continue their legal challenge against the retailer regarding the alleged contamination.
SBUX: This Cincinnati suburb is getting a $35M Kroger Marketplace | Going Up. A new Kroger Marketplace, costing $35 million and spanning approximately 123,000 square feet, is being built in West Chester Township, Cincinnati. It will replace an existing Kroger store, offering an expanded selection of groceries and non-grocery items, along with features like a Starbucks and a drive-thru pharmacy. The new store, which will create over 250 additional jobs, is expected to be completed by summer, after which the old store will be demolished.

Starbucks Settles COBRA Suit From Ex-Worker's Spouse. Starbucks has reached a settlement in a proposed class action lawsuit concerning alleged failures in its post-employment medical insurance notices. The suit, filed in Florida federal court, involved employee health plan participants and beneficiaries who claimed the coffee retailer fell short on its COBRA obligations.

Unusual Options Activity: 3 Multi-Leg Trades to Watch — SHOP, SBUX, and PINS. This article highlights unusual options activity for Shopify (SHOP), Starbucks (SBUX), and Pinterest (PINS), suggesting potential multi-leg options strategies for each. The author examines long strangle and long straddle trades for SHOP, a bull put spread for SBUX, and another long strangle for PINS, providing details on expected movements, breakeven points, and probabilities of profit. The analysis considers geopolitical tensions affecting the broader market and upcoming earnings reports for the featured stocks.

Water.org, Amazon & Ecolab: Driving Safe Water Sanitation. Water.org has launched its "Get Blue" initiative at the World Economic Forum's 2026 Annual Meeting in Davos, aiming to provide safe water and sanitation to 200 million people by 2030. The initiative partners with major companies like Amazon, Gap, Starbucks, and Ecolab to mobilize funding and resources. Addressing the global water crisis, "Get Blue" emphasizes cross-sector collaboration to scale solutions and improve health outcomes worldwide.

Starbucks Settles COBRA Notice Suit After Losing Arbitration Bid. Starbucks Corp. has agreed to settle a proposed class-action lawsuit concerning the health coverage notices it provides to former employees. The company and plaintiffs are finalizing the settlement paperwork and plan to submit it for court approval by February 19. The lawsuit alleged that Starbucks violated the Consolidated Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act (COBRA).

Starbucks class action alleges coffee not ethically sourced and contains industrial solvents. A new class action lawsuit has been filed against Starbucks, alleging that the company misleads consumers about the ethical sourcing of its coffee and conceals the presence of volatile organic compounds, including industrial solvents. Plaintiffs claim that despite Starbucks' assurances of "100% Ethical Coffee Sourcing," the brand has a history of documented labor abuses on C.A.F.E.-certified farms. The lawsuit also seeks disclosure of harmful chemicals like benzene and methylene chloride, which independent testing allegedly found in decaffeinated Starbucks coffee beans.
DIS: Adeia stock hits 52-week high at 19.57 USD By Investing.com. Adeia Inc. (ADEA) stock reached a 52-week high of $19.57, marking a 47.07% increase over the past year. InvestingPro data suggests the stock is undervalued with analyst price targets up to $27, and the company has a "GREAT" financial health score and a 1.04% dividend yield. Recent positive news includes a revised 2025 revenue guidance due to a new licensing agreement with The Walt Disney Company, despite missing Q3 2025 earnings and revenue forecasts.

Mattel, Disney and Viktor&Rolf Unveil Limited-Edition Couture Collector Doll Inspired by Disney’s Cinderella. Mattel, Disney, and Viktor&Rolf have collaborated to launch a limited-edition couture collector doll inspired by Disney's Cinderella. This unique doll, which debuted during Paris Fashion Week, features a dramatic haute couture gown and a smartphone set to 11:59 p.m., reimagining Cinderella as a modern, fashion-forward muse. The collaboration will also be featured in an upcoming documentary special, "Disney Princess: Create Your World – Mattel and Viktor&Rolf."

Adeia stock hits 52-week high at 19.57 USD By Investing.com. Adeia Inc. reached a new 52-week high of $19.57, marking a 47.07% increase over the past year. InvestingPro data suggests the stock is undervalued, with analysts setting price targets between $20 and $27. This growth follows a significant revenue guidance increase for 2025, driven by a new licensing agreement with The Walt Disney Company, despite a Q3 2025 earnings and revenue miss.

Rakuten Investment Management Inc. Takes $21.18 Million Position in The Walt Disney Company $DIS. Rakuten Investment Management Inc. acquired a new stake of 184,950 shares, valued at approximately $21.18 million, in The Walt Disney Company during Q3. Institutional ownership in Disney is substantial at 65.71%, with several firms increasing or initiating positions. Wall Street analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with an average target price of $135.20 for the stock.
NKE: Top 5 Stocks To Own In 2026: Dan Niles Bets On Apple, Cisco, Boeing And More. Tech investor Dan Niles predicts a "choppy" market in late 2026 but identifies significant upside in specific companies. He is bullish on Apple due to an anticipated AI-enabled Siri and foldable iPhone, Cisco as a value play in AI infrastructure, and Impinj for its RFID technology expansion. Additionally, Niles sees Boeing as a turnaround story with increased shipments and Nike benefiting from a "back to basics" strategy and undervalued stock.
